title: ICE Table Interactive Solver
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive ICE table that walks through Guided and Practice modes, evaluates the 5% approximation, and shows quadratic solving steps for equilibrium problems.

learningObjectives: Construct and interpret ICE tables with editable initial conditions.
Decide whether the small-x approximation is justified and solve for x using the quadratic formula when needed.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Guided problem solving with practice mode feedback
keyQuestions: When is the 5% approximation acceptable for an ICE setup?
How do initial concentrations and K values influence the size of x?
commonMisconceptions: The small-x approximation is always valid when K is small.
Quadratic solutions automatically produce both physically meaningful concentrations.
assessmentOpportunities: Students capture screenshots of completed Practice Mode tables.
Learners justify the approximation verdict with percent-change calculations.
